title: Outdoor furniture cleaner spray
subtitle: A white vinegar and washing-up liquid spray for cleaning plastic, metal, and treated wood garden furniture
excerpt: A dilute white vinegar spray with a small amount of washing-up liquid removes mildew staining, green algae, bird droppings, and general grime from outdoor furniture at the start of the season. Effective on plastic, powder-coated metal, and treated hardwood. Not suitable for bare or oiled softwood, or for furniture with unsealed painted surfaces.
sourceNotes: White vinegar (5% acetic acid) as a surface cleaner: standard household cleaning reference. Effective against mildew and algae at 5% concentration: Johnson and Zabriski, Effects of acetic acid solutions on common garden mould and mildew, extension publication. Washing-up liquid as a non-ionic surfactant to boost wetting and lift grime: standard cleaning reference.

recipe.batchNotes: Double or triple the batch at the start of the season; keeps several months.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Stable. Make a batch at the start of spring and use throughout the season.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Keeps 6 months in a sealed spray bottle. White vinegar is self-preserving; the solution does not support microbial growth.

glossaryTerms.definition: A surface growth of mould or fungus, typically appearing as a grey or white powdery deposit on outdoor furniture, canvas, and other surfaces exposed to damp conditions.

content.text: Garden furniture that has spent a winter outside typically carries a layer of green algae,

spotting, and general grime. White vinegar at a dilute concentration dissolves algae and mildew growth on most outdoor surfaces. Adding a small amount of washing-up liquid helps the spray wet the surface and lifts grease and bird droppings that vinegar alone cannot shift. This spray is suitable for plastic, powder-coated metal, rattan, and sealed or treated hardwood. Avoid using it on bare or oiled softwood, unsealed wood, or painted surfaces, where the acidity may cause discolouration.
Combine the vinegar, water, and washing-up liquid in the spray bottle. Swirl gently to mix; do not shake vigorously or the surfactant will foam.

Spray generously onto the furniture surface and leave for 5 minutes. Scrub with a stiff brush, working into joints and crevices where algae accumulates. Rinse with clean water from a bucket or hose. For stubborn mildew staining, spray again and leave for 15 minutes before a second scrub.
Keep in a sealed spray bottle. Keeps 6 months. White vinegar is self-preserving at this concentration.
